Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page. Join the Muslim Center of Greater Princeton on our journey to grow, empower, and serve our community Asalaamualaikum MCGP Family, Alhamdulillah, the MCGP team has worked diligently to clean the primary parking lot and 379 lot. While the spaces have been cleared, conditions remain less than ideal and parking capacity is limited. For safety reasons, we do not recommend full community attendance for Isha and Taraweeh at this time. Isha and a shortened 8 rakat Taraweeh will be conducted. We strongly encourage elders and children to remain at home. 🤲 Isha salaah will be at 8pm, inshallah This Ramadan, come pray, reflect, and grow together as one ummah. Prayer times will be at 7am (Takbeerat 6:45am), 9am (Takbeerat 8:45am) & 11am ( Takbeerat 10:45am). We urge guests to bring their own shoe bags for more efficient check in & exit. title: Monthly community open house
description: The Islamic Center of Union County is pleased to invite you, your friends and families to our monthly community open house events.
raw_text: Outreach — Islamic Center of Union County Sign In My Account Summer Camp Registration 2025 About Us Mission & Vision What We Do Imam Our Team Gallery Contact Us Follow Us Make a Payment Live IMAM Our Services MATRIMONIAL MUSLIM BURIAL More Services Latest News New Events Our Calendar Community News Join Our Email List Programs Sisters Classes Brothers Classes Family Class Youth Kids Club Volunteer with us Schools Al-Mishkat Sunday School Al-Mizmar Quran Academy Al-Thahabiya Quran School Donate Now Donate Sign In My Account Summer Camp Registration 2025 About Us Mission & Vision What We Do Imam Our Team Gallery Contact Us Follow Us Make a Payment Live IMAM Our Services MATRIMONIAL MUSLIM BURIAL More Services Latest News New Events Our Calendar Community News Join Our Email List Programs Sisters Classes Brothers Classes Family Class Youth Kids Club Volunteer with us Schools Al-Mishkat Sunday School Al-Mizmar Quran Academy Al-Thahabiya Quran School Donate Now Donate DAWAH & OUTREACH PROGRAMS Open house The Islamic Center of Union County is pleased to invite you, your friends and families to our monthly community open house events. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page.
